children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise specifically provided in this section, the provisions of chapter 84 of title 5 shall apply in the case of an employee of the Agency who is subject to chapter 84 of title 5 and who has a former spouse (as defined in ) or a qualified former spouse.section 8401(12) of title 5

children.heading: Employee
children.label: 1 Employee
children.text: The term “employee” means an employee of the Agency who is subject to chapter 84 of title 5, including an employee referred to in .section 2152(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a former spouse whose divorce from such employee became final on or before , was married to such employee for not less than 10 years during periods of the employee’s service which are creditable under , at least 5 years of which were spent outside the United States by both the employee and the former spouse during the employee’s service with the Agency; andDecember 4, 1991section 8411 of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a former spouse whose divorce from such employee becomes final after , was married to such employee for not less than 10 years during periods of the employee’s service which are creditable under , at least 5 years of which were spent by the employee outside the United States during the employee’s service with the Agency or otherwise in a position the duties of which qualified the employee for designation by the Director under the criteria prescribed in .December 4, 1991section 8411 of title 5section 2013 of this title

children.heading: Pro rata share
children.label: 3 Pro rata share
children.text: The term “pro rata share” means the percentage that is equal to (A) the number of days of the marriage of the qualified former spouse to the employee during the employee’s periods of creditable service under chapter 84 of title 5, divided by (B) the total number of days of the employee’s creditable service.

children.label: A
children.text: is in writing, is signed by the parties, and is notarized;

children.label: B
children.text: has not been modified by court order; and

children.label: C
children.text: has been authenticated by the Director.

children.heading: Court order
children.label: 5 Court order
children.text: The term “court order” means any court decree of divorce, annulment or legal separation, or any court order or court-approved property settlement agreement incident to such court decree of divorce, annulment, or legal separation.

children.heading: Entitlement of qualified former spouse to retirement benefits
children.label: c Entitlement of qualified former spouse to retirement benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Unless otherwise expressly provided by a spousal agreement or court order governing disposition of benefits payable under subchapter II or V of chapter 84 of title 5, a qualified former spouse of an employee is entitled to a share (determined under subparagraph (B)) of all benefits otherwise payable to such employee under subchapter II or V of chapter 84 of title 5.

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent, if the qualified former spouse was married to the employee throughout the entire period of the employee’s service which is creditable under chapter 84 of title 5; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a pro rata share of 50 percent, if the qualified former spouse was not married to the employee throughout such creditable service.

children.heading: Annuity supplement
children.label: 2 Annuity supplement
children.text: The benefits payable to an employee under subchapter II of chapter 84 of title 5 shall include, for purposes of this subsection, any annuity supplement payable to such employee under sections 8421 and 8421a of such title.

children.heading: Disqualification upon remarriage before age 55
children.label: 3 Disqualification upon remarriage before age 55
children.text: A qualified former spouse shall not be entitled to any benefit under this subsection if, before the commencement of any benefit, the qualified former spouse remarries before becoming 55 years of age.

children.label: i
children.text: the day on which the employee upon whose service the benefits are based becomes entitled to the benefits; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the first day of the second month beginning after the date on which the Director receives written notice of the court order or spousal agreement, together with such additional information or documentation as the Director may prescribe.

children.label: i
children.text: the last day of the month before the qualified former spouse remarries before 55 years of age or dies; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the retired employee’s benefits terminate (except in the case of benefits subject to paragraph (5)(B)).

children.label: i
children.text: the survivor annuity for any spouse, former spouse (qualified or otherwise), or other survivor under chapter 84 of title 5, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any reduction in the annuity of the retired employee to provide survivor benefits under subsection (d) of this section or under section 8442 or 8445 of title 5.

children.heading: Reduction in basic pay upon recall to service
children.label: B Reduction in basic pay upon recall to service
children.text: If a retired employee whose annuity is reduced under paragraph (1) is recalled to service under , the basic pay of that annuitant shall be reduced by the same amount as the annuity would have been reduced if it had continued. Amounts equal to the reductions under this subparagraph shall be deposited in the Treasury of the United States to the credit of the Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund.section 2152(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the annuity of such former spouse shall commence on the date on which the employee would qualify, on the basis of the employee’s creditable service, for benefits under subchapter II of chapter 84 of title 5 or on the date on which the disability annuity begins, whichever is later; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of the annuity of the qualified former spouse shall be calculated on the basis of the benefits for which the employee would otherwise qualify under subchapter II of chapter 84 of such title.

children.heading: Pro rata share in case of employees transferred to FERS
children.label: 7 Pro rata share in case of employees transferred to FERS
A
children.text: 50 percent of the employee’s annuity under subchapter III of chapter 83 of title 5 or under subchapter II of this chapter (computed in accordance with section 302(a) of the Federal Employees’ Retirement System Act of 1986 or ), multiplied by the proportion that the number of days of marriage during the period of the employee’s creditable service before the effective date of the election to transfer bears to the employee’s total creditable service before such effective date; andsection 2157 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: if applicable, 50 percent of the employee’s benefits under chapter 84 of title 5 or (computed in accordance with section 302(a) of the Federal Employees’ Retirement System Act of 1986 or ), multiplied by the proportion that the number of days of marriage during the period of the employee’s creditable service on and after the effective date of the election to transfer bears to the employee’s total creditable service after such effective date.section 2152(a) of this titlesection 2157 of this title

children.heading: Treatment of pro rata share under title 26
children.label: 8 Treatment of pro rata share under title 26
children.text: For purposes of title 26, payments to a qualified former spouse under this subsection shall be treated as income to the qualified former spouse and not to the employee.

children.heading: Qualified former spouse survivor benefits
children.label: d Qualified former spouse survivor benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to an election under , and unless otherwise expressly provided by any spousal agreement or court order governing survivor benefits payable under this subsection to a qualified former spouse, such former spouse is entitled to a share, determined under subparagraph (B), of all survivor benefits that would otherwise be payable under subchapter IV of chapter 84 of title 5 to an eligible surviving spouse of the employee.section 8416(a) of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: 100 percent, if the qualified former spouse was married to the employee throughout the entire period of the employee’s service which is creditable under chapter 84 of title 5; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a pro rata share of 100 percent, if the qualified former spouse was not married to the employee throughout such creditable service.

children.label: A
children.text: The survivor benefits payable under this subsection to a qualified former spouse shall include the amount payable under and any supplementary annuity under section 8442(f) of such title that would be payable if such former spouse were a widow or widower entitled to an annuity under such section.section 8442(b)(1)(A) of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: Any calculation under of the supplementary annuity payable to a widow or widower of an employee referred to in shall be based on an “assumed CIARDS annuity” rather than an “assumed CSRS annuity” as stated in section 8442(f) of such title. For the purpose of this subparagraph, the term “assumed CIARDS annuity” means the amount of the survivor annuity to which the widow or widower would be entitled under subchapter II of this chapter based on the service of the deceased annuitant determined under section 8442(f)(5) of such title.section 8442(f) of title 5section 2152(a) of this title

children.heading: Disqualification upon remarriage before age 55
children.label: 3 Disqualification upon remarriage before age 55
children.text: A qualified former spouse shall not be entitled to any benefit under this subsection if, before commencement of any benefit, the qualified former spouse remarries before becoming 55 years of age.

children.label: A
children.text: such former spouse elects to receive this survivor annuity instead of any other survivor benefit to which such former spouse may be entitled under subchapter IV of chapter 84 of title 5, or under another retirement system for Government employees by reason of the remarriage; and

children.label: B
children.text: any lump sum paid on termination of the annuity is returned to the Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund.

children.heading: Modification of court order or spousal agreement
children.label: 5 Modification of court order or spousal agreement
children.text: A modification in a court order or spousal agreement to adjust a qualified former spouse’s share of the survivor benefits shall not be effective if issued after the retirement or death of the employee, former employee, or annuitant, whichever occurs first.

children.heading: Effect of termination of qualified former spouse’s entitlement
children.label: 6 Effect of termination of qualified former spouse’s entitlement
children.text: After a qualified former spouse of a retired employee remarries before becoming age 55 or dies, the reduction in the retired employee’s annuity for the purpose of providing a survivor annuity for such former spouse shall be terminated. The annuitant may elect, in a signed writing received by the Director within 2 years after the qualified former spouse’s remarriage or death, to continue the reduction in order to provide or increase the survivor annuity for such annuitant’s spouse. The annuitant making such election shall pay a deposit in accordance with the provisions of .section 8418 of title 5

children.heading: Pro rata share in case of employees transferred to FERS
children.label: 7 Pro rata share in case of employees transferred to FERS
A
children.text: 50 percent of the employee’s annuity under subchapter III of chapter 83 of title 5 or under subchapter II of this chapter (computed in accordance with section 302(a) of the Federal Employees’ Retirement System Act of 1986 or ), multiplied by the proportion that the number of days of marriage during the period of the employee’s creditable service before the effective date of the election to transfer bears to the employee’s total creditable service before such effective date; andsection 2157 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent of the employee’s annuity under chapter 84 of title 5 or (computed in accordance with section 302(a) of the Federal Employees’ Retirement System Act of 1986 or ), plussection 2152(a) of this titlesection 2157 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the survivor benefits referred to in subsection (d)(2)(A),

children.heading: Qualified former spouse Thrift Savings Plan benefit
children.label: e Qualified former spouse Thrift Savings Plan benefit

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Unless otherwise expressly provided by a spousal agreement or court order governing disposition of the balance of an account in the Thrift Savings Fund under subchapter III of chapter 84 of title 5, a qualified former spouse of an employee is entitled to a share (determined under subparagraph (B)) of the balance in the employee’s account in the Thrift Savings Fund on the date the divorce of the qualified former spouse and employee becomes final.

children.heading: Amount of share
children.label: B Amount of share
children.text: The share referred to in subparagraph (A) equals 50 percent of the employee’s account balance in the Thrift Savings Fund that accrued during the period of marriage. For purposes of this subsection, the employee’s account balance shall not include the amount of any outstanding loan.

children.heading: Time of payment
children.label: A Time of payment
children.text: The entitlement of a qualified former spouse under paragraph (1) shall be effective on the date the divorce of the qualified former spouse and employee becomes final. The qualified former spouse’s benefit shall be payable after the date on which the Director receives the divorce decree or any applicable court order or spousal agreement, together with such additional information or documentation as the Director may require.

children.heading: Method of payment
children.label: B Method of payment
children.text: The qualified former spouse’s benefit under this subsection shall be paid in a lump sum.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: A spousal agreement or court order may not provide for payment to a qualified former spouse under this subsection of an amount that exceeds the employee’s account balance in the Thrift Savings Fund.

children.heading: Death of qualified former spouse
children.label: D Death of qualified former spouse
children.text: If the qualified former spouse dies before payment of the benefit provided under this subsection, such payment shall be made to the estate of the qualified former spouse.

children.heading: Bar to recovery
children.label: E Bar to recovery
children.text: Any payment under this subsection to an individual bars recovery by any other individual.

children.heading: Closed account
children.label: 3 Closed account
children.text: No payment under this subsection may be made by the Director if the date on which the divorce becomes final is after the date on which the total amount of the employee’s account balance has been withdrawn or transferred, or the date on which an annuity contract has been purchased, in accordance with .section 8433 of title 5

children.heading: Preservation of rights of qualified former spouses
children.label: f Preservation of rights of qualified former spouses
children.text: An employee may not make an election or modification of election under section 8417 or 8418 of title 5, or other section relating to the employee’s annuity under subchapter II of chapter 84 of title 5, that would diminish the entitlement of a qualified former spouse to any benefit granted to such former spouse by this section or by court order or spousal agreement.

children.heading: Payment of share of lump-sum credit
children.label: g Payment of share of lump-sum credit
children.text: Whenever an employee or former employee becomes entitled to receive the lump-sum credit under , a share (determined under subsection (c)(1)(B) of this section) of that lump-sum credit shall be paid to any qualified former spouse of such employee, unless otherwise expressly provided by any spousal agreement or court order governing disposition of the lump-sum credit involved.section 8424(a) of title 5

children.heading: Payment to qualified former spouses under court order or spousal agreement
children.label: h Payment to qualified former spouses under court order or spousal agreement

children.text: any right of the qualified former spouse to any retirement benefits under subsection (c) and to any survivor benefits under subsection (d), and the amount of any such benefits;
any right of the qualified former spouse to any Thrift Savings Plan benefit under subsection (e), and the amount of any such benefit; and
any right of the qualified former spouse to any payment of a lump-sum credit under subsection (g), and the amount of any such payment;

children.heading: Applicability of CIARDS former spouse benefits
children.label: i Applicability of CIARDS former spouse benefits

children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), in the case of an employee who has elected to become subject to chapter 84 of title 5, the provisions of sections 2034 and 2035 of this title shall apply to such employee’s former spouse (as defined in ) who would otherwise be eligible for benefits under sections 2034 and 2035 of this title but for the employee having elected to become subject to such chapter.section 2002(a)(4) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the retirement benefits shall be equal to the amount determined under subsection (c)(7)(A); and

children.label: B
children.text: the survivor benefits shall be equal to 55 percent of the full amount of the employee’s annuity computed in accordance with section 302(a) of the Federal Employees’ Retirement System Act of 1986 or regulations prescribed under .section 2157 of this title
Benefits provided pursuant to this subsection shall be payable from the Central Intelligence Agency Retirement and Disability Fund.
